mission.title: kube-ovn: Support for using Talos Linux
mission.description: Support for using Talos Linux. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kube-ovn troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your Kube-OVN deployment on Talos Linux:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=kube-ovn
kubectl logs -l app=ovs -n kube-system --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to module loading or filesystem access on Talos Linux.

steps.title: Review kube-ovn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the Kube-OVN configuration on Talos Linux:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=kube-ovn
kubectl get nodes -o wide
```
Talos Linux has a read-only filesystem except for `/var`. Kube-OVN requires write access to directories like `/etc/origin/openvswitch`, `/opt/cni/bin`, and `/etc/cni/net.d`, which are read-only on Talos.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for using Talos Linux
steps.description:
The fix involves bind-mounting Kube-OVN's required directories to writable paths under `/var` on Talos Linux.

Key error on Talos:
```
modprobe: FATAL: Module openvswitch not found in directory /lib/modules/6.1.23-talos
nsenter: cannot open /var/run/netns/*: No such file or directory
ovs-vswitchd is not running
```

Workaround: Talos machine config needs to include extensions or bind mounts for the required kernel modules and writable paths. The `kubectl-ko` tool should also be bundled in the container images rather than requiring installation at `/usr/local/bin`.

See the source issue for community discussion: https://github.com/kubeovn/kube-ovn/issues/2707

steps.title: Confirm Support for using Talos Linux is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that Kube-OVN pods are running on Talos Linux:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=kube-ovn
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=ovs
kubectl logs -l app=ovs -n kube-system --tail=50 --since=5m
```
Confirm that OVS and OVN components start correctly without filesystem errors.

resolution.summary:
>  According to the [documentation](https://www.talos.dev/v1.4/learn-more/architecture/#the-file-system) only /var is fully writable

 Thanks for the information, there are also other directories that Kube-OVN need to write like `/etc/origin`, `/opt/cni`, `/etc/cni` and so on. I think it's more suitable to bindmount these directories to the `/var`

nsenter: cannot open /var/run/netns/*: No such file or directory

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kube-ovn installed or the issue environment reproducible.
